title: Sucrose self-administration protocol
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"85r5p","text":"All procedures were carried out following the guidelines on the ethical use of animals from the Council Directive of the European Communities (2010/63/EU) and were approved by the Italian Ministry of Health","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Train the rats in a self-administration chamber equipped with a stainless-steel grid ﬂoor andtwo operant panels placed on the left and right walls, including the palatablesolution-paired active (retractable) lever

Train the rats for two-bottle choice pre-conditioning for 3 days: house the rats individually with twobottles containing water (habituation) (first day) and then replace a bottle of water with another one containing a sucrose solution at 2.5% and maintain this configuration for two days (second and third day)
Train the rats to self-administer the sucrose solution 1 hour/day for 8 days (first and second day were trained for 3h) at fixed ratio 1 (FR1) with no timeout
Measure the sucrose intake of each rat for the two consecutive days and then normalize for the body weight

Test the rats for the progressive ratio (PR) for three hours. To this end, the number of lever presses (ratio value) required for drug delivery is incremented within sessions and different days following this progression: 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 12, 15, 20, 25, 32, 40, 50, 62, 77, 95, 118, 145, 178, 219, 268, 328, 402, 492,603, etc.
Leave the rats in the behavioral room for acclimation for at least 1 hour before the test, and perform the marble burying test between 9:00 am and 16:00 pm.
creator: Ada Ledonne
